title: Middleware in Next.js

learningObjectives.text: Trace and explain request interception using a new example and justify the result.
Explain redirects and rewrites using a new example and justify the result.
Explain common middleware use cases using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Middleware in Next.js is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Server and Client Architecture** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Request interception** → **Redirects and rewrites** → **Common middleware use cases**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Request interception
sections.body: Middleware runs before matching route handling and can inspect or modify requests and responses.

sections.title: Redirects and rewrites
sections.body: A redirect changes the client's URL; a rewrite serves a different destination while preserving the visible URL.

sections.title: Common middleware use cases
sections.body: Use middleware for lightweight redirects, locale routing, or coarse gates. In newer Next.js versions the feature may be called Proxy; Release 1 targets Next 15.5.20 terminology.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: For the pinned Next 15 project, middleware intercepts and redirects a request.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.
summary: Middleware in Next.js connects Request interception, Redirects and rewrites, Common middleware use cases.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Which statement best explains “Request interception” in the context of Middleware in Next.js?

choices.label: Middleware runs before matching route handling and can inspect or modify requests and responses.
choices.feedback: Request interception is best understood as follows: Middleware runs before matching route handling and can inspect or modify requests and responses.

choices.label: Server execution can protect secrets, keep data access close to its source, reduce client JavaScript, and support streaming or caching.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Request interception.

choices.label: Forms can invoke Server Actions while preserving progressive enhancement. Validate authorization and input on the server.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Request interception.

choices.label: Use props for server-to-client data, URLs for shareable navigation state, server mutations for persistence, and client state for interaction.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Request interception.

questions.hint: Focus on what the concept changes, enables, or constrains—not only on its syntax.

choices.label: Add more state and abstraction immediately, before identifying the smallest requirement.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

choices.label: Skip the underlying model and rely on memorized syntax, even when the result is unclear.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

choices.label: Apply Request interception deliberately, make the relevant inputs and assumptions explicit, and verify the observable result before adding more complexity.
choices.feedback: Transfer comes from using the concept with explicit assumptions and checking the resulting behavior, not from reproducing a memorized example.

choices.label: Treat the concept as isolated trivia and avoid connecting it to earlier prerequisites.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

questions.prompt: Which sequence is most reliable when solving a problem involving Middleware in Next.js?

choices.label: Optimize first, then decide what the code is supposed to do.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

choices.label: Copy an unrelated implementation, hide the assumptions, and test only the happy path.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Request interception, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.
choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.

choices.label: Start with a framework abstraction and avoid checking the underlying language behavior.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

questions.explanation: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
questions.hint: Prefer a sequence that makes failures visible early.

questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best judgment about when to use Middleware in Next.js?

choices.label: Avoid Middleware in Next.js completely because all abstractions reduce maintainability.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

choices.label: Choose based only on line count, without considering readability, correctness, or future change.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

choices.label: Use Middleware in Next.js when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.
choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.

choices.label: Use Middleware in Next.js in every file because more abstraction is always better.
choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Middleware in Next.js.

questions.explanation: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
questions.hint: Choose the option that acknowledges both benefits and trade-offs.
